name: Antigua and Barbuda
excerpt: The Siboney were the first people to inhabit the islands of Antigua and Barbuda in 2400 B.C., but the Arawaks populated the islands when Christopher COLUMBUS landed on his second voyage in 1493. Early Spanish and French settlements were succeeded by an English colony in 1667.

quick_facts.area: 443 sq km (Antigua 280 sq km; Barbuda 161 sq km)
quick_facts.government: parliamentary democracy under a constitutional monarchy; a Commonwealth realm
quick_facts.religions: Protestant 68.3% (Anglican 17.6%, Seventh Day Adventist 12.4%, Pentecostal 12.2%, Moravian 8.3%, Methodist 5.6%, Wesleyan Holiness 4.5%, Church of God 4.1%, Baptist 3.6%), Roman Catholic 8.2%, other 12.2%, unspecified 5.5%, none 5.9% (2011 est.)
quick_facts.ethnic_groups: African descent 87.3%, mixed 4.7%, Hispanic 2.7%, White 1.6%, other 2.7%, unspecified 0.9% (2011 est.)
quick_facts.climate: tropical maritime; little seasonal temperature variation
quick_facts.terrain: mostly low-lying limestone and coral islands, with some higher volcanic areas
quick_facts.major_exports: refined petroleum, ships, soybean meal, shellfish, paintings (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: Antigua has a deeply indented shoreline with many natural harbors and beaches; Barbuda has a large western harbor
summaries.history_summary: The Siboney were the first people to inhabit the islands of Antigua and Barbuda in 2400 B.C., but the Arawaks populated the islands when Christopher COLUMBUS landed on his second voyage in 1493. Early Spanish and French settlements were succeeded by an English colony in 1667. Slavery, which provided labor on the sugar plantations on Antigua, was abolished in 1834. The islands became an independent state within the British Commonwealth of Nations in 1981. In 2017, Hurricane Irma passed over the island of Barbuda, devastating the island and forcing the evacuation of the population to Antigua. Almost all of the structures on Barbuda were destroyed and the vegetation stripped, but Antigua was spared the worst.
summaries.government_summary: parliamentary democracy under a constitutional monarchy; a Commonwealth realm. Independence: 1 November 1981 (from the UK)
summaries.economy_summary: dual island-tourism and construction-driven economy; emerging “blue economy”; limited water supply and susceptibility to hurricanes limit activity; improving road infrastructure; friendly to foreign direct investment; looking at financial innovation in cryptocurrency and blockchain technologies

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: Antigua and Barbuda already has a strong Protestant presence, including an existing Baptist witness, giving real ground to build on rather than start from nothing. Pray for these churches to be strengthened in sound biblical doctrine.
prayer_points: Pray for Antigua and Barbuda's existing Baptist and Protestant churches to be strengthened and multiplied.
Pray for pastors to be trained in expository, Scripture-centered preaching.
Pray for genuine gospel conversion rather than inherited church membership across the islands.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: With a substantial Protestant majority including an existing Baptist presence, Antigua and Barbuda has real ground to build on. Ongoing needs include strengthening biblical literacy among existing churches and ensuring church membership reflects genuine, personal faith in Christ rather than inherited tradition.
